# Proof that Each Uniformity is Generated by Uniformly Continuous Pseudo-Metrics (Theorem 6.15)

**Theorem 6.15.** Each uniformity $\mathcal{U}$ for $X$ is generated by the family of all pseudo-metrics which are uniformly continuous on $X \times X$.

**Proof.** Let $P$ be the family of all pseudo-metrics for $X$ which are uniformly continuous on $X \times X$ relative to the product uniformity derived from $\mathcal{U}$. Let $\mathcal{U}_P$ be the uniformity generated by $P$ (i.e., the uniformity having as subbase all sets $V_{p,r} = \{(x,y) : p(x,y) < r\}$ for $p \in P$ and $r > 0$).

First, $\mathcal{U}_P \subset \mathcal{U}$: By Theorem 6.11, for each $p \in P$ we have $V_{p,r} \in \mathcal{U}$ for all $r > 0$. Hence every subbase member of $\mathcal{U}_P$ belongs to $\mathcal{U}$, so $\mathcal{U}_P \subset \mathcal{U}$.

Conversely, $\mathcal{U} \subset \mathcal{U}_P$: Let $U \in \mathcal{U}$. Set $U_0 = X \times X$ and by induction construct a sequence $\{U_n\}$ of symmetric members of $\mathcal{U}$ such that $U_{n+1} \circ U_{n+1} \circ U_{n+1} \subset U_n$ and $U_1 \subset U$. By the Metrization Lemma (6.12), there exists a pseudo-metric $d$ such that

$$U_2 \subset \{(x,y) : d(x,y) < 1/4\} \subset U_1 \subset U.$$

Since $V_{d,1/4} \in \mathcal{U}$, Theorem 6.11 implies $d$ is uniformly continuous on $X \times X$, so $d \in P$. But $V_{d,1/4} \subset U$, hence $U \in \mathcal{U}_P$ (as it contains a member of the uniformity generated by $P$).

Therefore $\mathcal{U} = \mathcal{U}_P$, i.e., $\mathcal{U}$ is generated by $P$.
